Nine pebbles, nine grahas: order and cha: Nine pebbles, nine grahas: order and chaos each try to align the heavens and unmake the other's design.
Navakankari means 'nine pebbles'. Here t: Navakankari means 'nine pebbles'. Here they are the nine grahas, and two cosmic orders vie to bring the heavens into alignment — three lights in a line hold, and dim one of the other's.
The board is the wheel of the sky. To al: The board is the wheel of the sky. To align three is to fix an order; to be aligned against is to fall dark.
Three lights align — a constellation hol: Three lights align — a constellation holds. In the old cosmology, alignment is order itself asserting over chaos.
A line of three completes. The subtle ar: A line of three completes. The subtle art: seat a light where two constellations can form, so one is unstoppable.
The heavens lock into pattern. Foresight: The heavens lock into pattern. Foresight here is prophecy — you saw the alignment before it was.
A rival light is dimmed — though never o: A rival light is dimmed — though never one already fixed in a constellation, unless all are. Extinguish the one that anchored their design.
One graha falls dark. Unmake the light t: One graha falls dark. Unmake the light that held their pattern together, and the sky is yours to redraw.
Only two of the rival's lights remain; n: Only two of the rival's lights remain; no alignment can form. The heavens have settled into your order.
Two lights, and no constellation left to: Two lights, and no constellation left to draw. The other order has fixed the heavens against you.
